raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I paid {$140.00} to XXXX XXXX for the replacement of tire pressure sensors ( TPMS ). During the service, the merchant caused physical damage to my vehicle 's fender flare. Despite providing photographic evidence, American Express ( Amex ) has repeatedly denied my dispute claims. 

I arrived at XXXX XXXX with my vehicle in good condition. Upon completion of the sensor installation, I immediately discovered damage to the damage that was not present before the service. I have time-stamped " Before '' and " After '' photographs clearly documenting this damage. The shop manager refused to acknowledge the damage or provide compensation for repairs.

I initiated a dispute with American Express, providing the photographic evidence of the damage. Amex rejected the dispute, stating that " the service was rendered '' because the sensors were installed. 

I have attempted to re-dispute this charge multiple times, explaining that the quality of service was negligent and resulted in a net financial loss due to property damage. Amex has failed to conduct a " reasonable investigation '' into the quality of the service as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ).

Actions Taken : I attempted to resolve the issue directly with the XXXX XXXX manager at the time of the incident but was ignored. 
I also emailed their manager more than three times without receiving any response.

I provided Amex with visual proof of the damage.

Amex continues to prioritize the merchant 's claim of " delivery '' over the documented evidence yof " negligent service and property damage.
